research.note: High-priority maintenance research opened from the v4.3 cleanup program. Scope: workout reminders, readiness alerts, recovery/trend alerts, and notification preferences.

transitions.note: Research chapter opened after the maintenance prioritization pass identified push notifications as a top high-priority product gap.
Research phase complete. All scope questions answered. Ready for PRD authoring.
PRD written with 17 requirements, 5 metrics, 10 analytics events
12 tasks defined. Critical path: NotificationService → types → deep links → tests.
UX spec: permission priming, 3 notification types, timing rules, DS compliance.
BUILD SUCCEEDED. Tests pass. Ready for review.
C1+C2+I1 fixed. Review re-approved. First feature to complete full v5.2 PM lifecycle.
Reopened 2026-04-20 per UI-016: priming view shipped tested + merged but never wired into navigation. Partial ship flagged per open-items review § #12.

timing.backfill_note: Phase boundaries inferred from state.json transitions[] array; timestamps from git log. T15 / PR-5 (2026-04-27).

implementation_first.note: First implementation pass (pre-audit). Completed, then reopened for partial-ship fix.

known_gaps.summary: Local-notification substrate + scheduler + 2 critical-bug fixes + 18 tests shipped cleanly. NotificationPermissionPrimingView was built, tested, and merged but was never wired into navigation — the first-workout-completed trigger was never implemented, so zero entry points exist. No user has seen the priming surface.
